frameworks.name: deep generative models / normalizing flows
frameworks.one_line: Deep probability distributions as flexible approximating family for circuit parameter posteriors.

frameworks.name: approximate Bayesian computation
frameworks.one_line: Conventional ABC for circuit inverse problems, contrasted on scaling and expressivity.

frameworks.name: maximum entropy / variational inference
frameworks.one_line: EPI optimization framed as max-entropy distribution under emergent-property moment constraints.

frameworks.name: stabilized supralinear network (E/I balance)
frameworks.one_line: Used as primary visual cortex model whose parametric structure EPI dissects.

core_question: How can one infer distributions of circuit-model parameters that produce a specified emergent computational property, rather than fitting an exemplar dataset?
key_assumptions: Computation of interest is expressible as moment constraints on a derived statistic (the emergent property).
A deep normalizing-flow family is expressive enough to capture multimodal, curved parameter posteriors.
Maximum-entropy selection over constraint-satisfying distributions yields a principled posterior.
manipulations: Circuit model studied (STG subcircuit, rank-2 RNN, V1 SSN, superior colliculus task-switching).
Parameter dimensionality / network size N (scalability test vs ABC and SNPE).
Specified emergent property (intermediate hub frequency, stable amplification, excitatory variability, task-switching connectivity).
key_findings: EPI scales to higher parameter dimension and converges more efficiently than ABC and SNPE on RNN connectivity inference.
In V1 SSN, EPI exposes how recurrent processing across neuron-type inputs shapes excitatory variability — a relationship shown to be analytically intractable.
In superior colliculus, EPI discovers two distinct connectivity regimes for task switching that reproduce optogenetic-inactivation results in different ways.
Hessian of the learned log-density at modes yields per-direction sensitivity/robustness of parameter combinations to the emergent property.
interpretations: Emergent-property inference, by conditioning directly on the computation rather than a dataset, is better aligned with theorists' modeling goals than likelihood-on-data fitting.
Deep inference makes previously intractable interrogation of biologically realistic circuit models — including degeneracy, multimodality, and sensitivity structure — routine.
limitations_noted: Specifying an emergent property as moment constraints requires the theorist to articulate the computation statistically, which may be non-trivial.
